item.title: B-Sides and mostly lame stuff.
item.dc:creator: RestingVikingFace

item.description: Ingredients: 1.5 lbs (680g) sirloin steak, cut into 1-inch cubes. Salt and pepper, to taste. 2 tablespoons olive oil. 4 tablespoons unsalted butter, divided. 6 cloves garlic, minced. 1 teaspoon fresh thyme leaves. 1 teaspoon fresh rosemary leaves, chopped. 1 teaspoon fresh parsley, chopped. 1 tablespoon butter. 13 cloves garlic, minced. 1 cup heavy cream. - ½ cup grated Parmesan cheese. Salt and pepper, to taste. 1 teaspoon Italian seasoning 2 - In a large skillet over medium-high heat, add olive oil and 2 tablespoons of butter. Once the butter has melted, add the steak in a single layer. Cook the steak bites for about 3-4 minutes, turning to brown all sides until they reach the desired doneness. Remove the steak from the skillet and set aside. In the same skillet, add the remaining 2 tablespoons of butter. Add the minced garlic, thyme, rosemary, and parsley, sautéing for about 1 minute until fragrant. Return the steak bites to the skillet, toss to coat them in the garlic herb butter, and cook for an additional 1-2 minutes. Remove from heat and set aside. In a separate skillet, melt 1 tablespoon of butter over medium heat. Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute until fragrant. Pour in the heavy cream and bring it to a gentle simmer. Stir occasionally. Add the grated Parmesan cheese and Italian seasoning, stirring until the cheese has melted and the sauce is smooth. Season with salt and pepper to taste. Allow the sauce to simmer for 2-3 minutes, stirring occasionally, until it thickens slightly. Pour the Parmesan cream sauce over the steak bites in the skillet or serve it on the side as a dipping sauce. Garnish with additional parsley, if desired, and serve immediately.
item.dc:creator: TheRainbowegoSweet007

item.dc:creator: bappoglibbus

item.title: the harpy's meme library: volume 65 (off to join the skeleton war)
item.description:
Hello and welcome to another meme dump, my imgur friends. I gotta say, it has been a week. Let us relax and enjoy some amusing images together.
I love all those things except sweet tea, that shit is nasty and you know it. Fight me, southerners.
He sure does, lucky for him he's SO CUTE and such a GOOD BOY
Yeah, it takes a lot of time and effort and shit to get that makeup right, I only bring out the full goth look for special occasions
I sure loved this show
Everything I know is against my will
Aww, beans
@CodyBurkett has no power over me because I happen to live in a part of California that rivals Arizona in terms of lethal heat. But the rest of you better watch out.
I'm the one on the right
I've come a long ways from my youth, I just spent the last few days in the Sims building a lot in painstaking detail even in the dark corners people probably won't even look. It's beautiful and I am proud of myself, easily the coolest thing I've ever made
Yeah I'm one of those people who needs vision insurance but doesn't have it, so like, seeing is fucking expensive, but I gotta see, you know? RIP my money
okie dokie
fuck yeah All right, that's all for this dump, my imgur friends. Until next time, don't forget to be awesome.
item.dc:creator: SecretHarpy

item.title: The question that my “undecided” neighbor asks shouldn’t be “why do you hate him?” I should be asking her why she doesn’t. She says she hates Harris’s laugh. I hate Trump’s fascism, felonies, and rapes.
item.description: Maybe not even then.

item.title: "There's some good in this world, Mr. Frodo, and it's worth fighting for"
item.description:
I work at a post-acute/rehabilitation center, lots of older folks. Recently we've been having a series of residents getting CoVID-19 infections, and so all nursing staff (yours truly included) have been working hard to follow precautions and wearing PPE when entering quarantined rooms. So you can imagine how pissed I feel over getting it myself. Eggothy Noodlebert, though, knows I'm not doing well, and has been actively comforting me during the last couple of days. I often joke that there's no thoughts in that head of his, but truly it's filled with nothing but love and care.
Edit 1: oh, hi again, Front Page! Edit 2: quite of people here seem to appreciate/are fascinated by Egg's name, so I figured I should let you all know his full, current name is Eggothy Thomas Noodlebert, Esquire; he shall accumulate more parts to his name as time passes and we witness more of his full personality. Edit 3: shameless Front Page plug, if you guys like sci-fi and fantasy you should check out my recent posts about my sci-fintasy setting; especially with how much people complain about seeing "too much politics", it wouldn't hurt to take a peek now would it? :3 1) https://imgur.com/gallery/lUBDfbU 2) https://imgur.com/gallery/bMUkPcE
item.dc:creator: goblinwithaproblem

item.title: Over the seas and it comes right over our land
item.dc:creator: KinseyH

item.title: It's been a hell of a week...
item.description:
Monday morning my wife took herself to the hospital because she was leaking fluid, she got admitted for preterm labor, and possible preeclampsia. The doctors planned to keep her on magnesium and steroids and to stall the labor until Wednesday. Tuesday during an ultrasound they find a huge mass in my son's chest that is squishing his heart and lungs (Google CPAM if you're curious). I miraculously finished my training at work early and arrived at the hospital shortly after they found out and I learned the news. Within half an hour my wife and I were being life flighted out of state to a hospital with a pediatric surgeon who deals with this issue regularly.
The flight was about an hour. Luckily it was in a fixed wing plane so no helicopter. It was still super cramped.
The plane is fully equipped with all the necessary life support and they were checking our baby's heart rate every 15 minutes.
Luckily my wife managed to get some sleep on the flight.
Pardon the selfie, but I did get to wear this nifty headset. Before we could land we had to wait for a passenger plane to make an emergency landing because they had an engine catch fire. Can you guess what kind of plane that was? If you guessed it was a Boeing, you're 100% correct!
My wife is a goddamn champion, and I couldn't be more proud of her. The continued with the medicine at the new hospital so she went through three days of off and on contractions while being on magnesium, having her vitals checked every few hours until they officially started an induction on Wednesday night to get us into active labor. All the while she gets no sleep and is still worried about me managing to get a decent amount of rest whenever I could. 17 more hours of active labor and our little boy was born at 5 lbs 1 oz.
We got to hold him for exactly one minute before I cut the cord and they passed him to the NICU.
Once he was stabilized they brought him over soy wife and I could see him before he was transported to the children's hospital across the way.
I was able to go with them but my wife was still recovering and had to wait. He spent Wednesday night and Thursday there getting around the clock care. Friday morning he went in for surgery. They removed the full upper lobe of his right lung where the mass was.
This was taken Friday after surgery. Everything went as well as we could have hoped. He is already improving so quickly and his heart lungs moved right back and the lungs inflated like they should. His vitals keep getting better and it looks like he's going to be a perfectly healthy boy eventually with no long lasting health issues. I haven't been able to hold him, and we won't be able to until he's off the ventilator, and that's likely not going to happen before I have to go back home for work. We're incredibly fortunate that my wife has maternity leave and FMLA so she'll be able to stay out here for the four to six weeks he'll need to stay in the NICU, and we got a room at the Ronald McDonald house so food and a place to sleep won't be an issue. I'll be able to stay out here for another week before I make the 6 hour drive back home. I'm dreading leaving them, my wife and I haven't been apart for more than a few days since we started dating, but we can't have lots of animals at home and with the medical costs of everything looming on the horizon, I can't afford to lose the job I just got. (For context I got laid off in March, normally I'm a Software and Data Engineer, but the job market has been a nightmare. Unemployment insurance ran out and I had to get a job delivering for Amazon to keep us afloat. I do have an interview next week for a super nice remote data job so fingers crossed the delivery gig doesn't last too long ) Anyway, this is probably going to be the longest month of my life. It's going to suck. However, I'm so grateful for all the care we've gotten, how fortunate we are that he's already recovering so well, so thankful that we have a place for my wife to stay for free while our boy is in the hospital, and talking with other families at the hospital, I know there's so much worse things that could happen to a child during development. One quick surgery and a few weeks in the NICU don't seem that bad by comparison. TLDR: son came early and needed surgery, but everything is going super well and he's going to make a full recovery
item.dc:creator: Necridos

item.title: Miranda Hart tells Selena Gomez that her documentary about her health struggles gave her motivation to keep working while she was dealing with her own chronic illness
item.dc:creator: hmo786
